title: App crashes on save with encoding error after CSV task import
problem: After importing tasks from a CSV file, clicking the Save button causes the application to immediately close. A dialog referencing 'encoding' flashes briefly before the app disappears. The user has approximately 200 tasks in the list. No freeze or gradual degradation — the app simply terminates.
root_cause_hypothesis: The CSV import likely introduced task data containing characters in an encoding (e.g., Latin-1, Windows-1252) that differs from the application's expected encoding (likely UTF-8). When the save routine attempts to serialize the task data, it encounters characters it cannot encode, throws an unhandled exception, and the application crashes. The briefly-visible dialog is likely the unhandled exception or encoding error surfacing before the process terminates.
reproduction_steps: Create or obtain a CSV file with tasks containing non-ASCII or mixed-encoding characters (e.g., accented characters, smart quotes, em-dashes)
Import the CSV file into TaskFlow
Click the Save button in the toolbar
Observe the app crashes with a brief encoding-related dialog
environment: Not specified — likely desktop application. OS and version not yet confirmed.
impact: The user cannot save their work at all after importing CSV data, leading to repeated data loss. Any user who imports CSV files with non-ASCII characters is likely affected. This is a data-loss scenario.
recommended_fix: Investigate the save/serialization code path for encoding assumptions. Ensure imported CSV data is normalized to a consistent encoding (UTF-8) at import time, or that the save routine handles mixed encodings gracefully. The unhandled exception should be caught and surfaced as a user-visible error rather than crashing the app. Specifically: (1) Add encoding detection/normalization in the CSV import pipeline, (2) Wrap the save serialization in proper error handling, (3) If encoding fails, show a meaningful error identifying which task(s) contain problematic characters.
proposed_test_case: Import a CSV file containing tasks with various non-ASCII characters (accented letters, CJK characters, smart quotes, emoji) and verify that saving completes successfully without crashing. Additionally, test with a CSV file explicitly saved in Latin-1 or Windows-1252 encoding to confirm the import normalizes encoding correctly.
information_gaps: The specific encoding of the source CSV file
Whether the crash occurs with a smaller subset of the imported tasks
Full text of the encoding error dialog
